domain: retail bank consumer credit-line decisioning: applicants requesting new credit lines or limit increases, their existing accounts, and simulated credit-bureau reports

personas.name: over-ceiling-stretch
personas.description: 38yo salaried professional, ~72k annual income, one existing credit card with a modest balance, clean 24-month delinquency record, credit score ~710. Requests a LIMIT INCREASE to 25,000 — well above the bank's 15,000 auto-approve ceiling — which also pushes debt-to-income just over appetite. Channel: app.

personas.name: clean-auto-approve
personas.description: 45yo homeowner, ~98k income, excellent credit score ~775, low outstanding debt, no delinquencies. Requests a NEW modest credit line around 8,000 — comfortably within the auto-approve ceiling and all thresholds. Channel: web.

personas.name: borderline-adverse
personas.description: 33yo self-employed renter, ~54k income, credit score in the high 600s, a couple of delinquencies in the last 24 months and several recent hard inquiries. Requests a small INCREASE around 6,000 that trips at least one policy threshold. Channel: branch.
